title: Cynanchica bryoides
taxonomy: Cynanchica bryoides is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to North America, where it can be found growing in woodland environments. The classification of Cynanchica bryoides is as follows: it belongs to the genus Cynanchica, which is a part of the Asclepiadoideae subfamily within the Apocynaceae family. Within the Cynanchica genus, Cynanchica bryoides is often grouped with other species such as Cynanchica stans and Cynanchica smithii. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and genetic makeup.

intro: Cynanchica bryoides, also known as the green-flowered twineflower or green-flowered vine, is a perennial flowering plant species within the Apocynaceae family. The health benefits of Cynanchica bryoides include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions, including anxiety and insomnia.
